title: How long are monday.com doc links valid after item or board deletion?

posts.author: ParthAtRewind (Parth)
posts.cookedHtml:
We are using monday.com documents attached to item columns via the “doc” column type. When a document is linked like this:

https://[workspace].monday.com/docs/[doc_id]

I’ve observed that even after an item is deleted—and then permanently deleted from the trash—the documents can be recovered if I have a stored JSON with the correct metadata. Since the document links appear to point to CDN-hosted content (assumption), I’m curious:

  • How long do these document links remain accessible after the item or board they belong to is permanently deleted?
  • Are the links eventually invalidated or removed from the CDN/Monday’s system?

Thanks in advance for shedding light on how monday.com handles the lifecycle of these document links!

posts.author: Meganotto (Megan O | Monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @ParthAtRewind, I don’t have a ton of knowledge in this area but this is what I’m finding:

  • There is no officially published TTL (Time to Live) or expiration period from Monday.com** for CDN document URLs after deletion.

My guess is those files may stick around until an internal cleanup job runs on the backend. If Monday.com uses S3 with lifecycle rules, cleanup could happen within 30–90 days.

posts.author: ParthAtRewind (Parth)
posts.cookedHtml:
image1222×380 24 KB

Seems like 30 days
